design_philosophy.core_loop: Create a party of D&D characters → explore dungeon areas from first-person perspective → engage in tactical turn-based combat → gain experience and level up → progress through increasingly challenging encounters toward confronting the evil leader
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Party resource management (hit points, spell memorization) balanced against tactical positioning in turn-based combat; risk of character death requiring active healing intervention
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Authentic tabletop D&D experience translated to computer: create and customize a full party following AD&D ruleset, make meaningful character build decisions (race/class/alignment), and control party tactics in real-time tactical combat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Faithful mechanical translation of AD&D ruleset rather than simplification; faithful monster depiction from Monster Manual; emphasis on party composition strategy over single-hero power fantasy; alignment system affects NPC interactions; random encounter tables mimic pen-and-paper experience

difficulty.lives_system: Character death system: if hit points drop below zero, character can be bandaged by another party member to prevent death; permadeath possible if all party members are incapacitated
difficulty.continues_system: Player-created save-game files allow continuation regardless of events; soft save system provides safety net
difficulty.checkpointing: Implicit area-based progression: player moves from one area to another clearing enemies; ability to save at any time provides manual checkpointing
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: players build party composition with meaningful race/class/alignment choices; optional auto-combat available for less mechanically engaged players; ability to reload saves mitigates consequence

progression.upgrade_system: Experience point system allowing characters to increase capabilities through leveling; spell memorization progression for magic-users and clerics; weapon and armor acquisition through gameplay
progression.unlock_mechanism: Class-based ability gating: fighter extra attacks and sweep attack unlock at higher levels; multi-classing available to non-human characters (with leveling penalty); spell access tied to character class and level
replayability_hooks: Character export to subsequent Gold Box games
Multiple race/class combinations with different mechanical capabilities
Multi-classing options for non-human characters enabling different builds
Alignment system creates different NPC interaction outcomes
Random encounter tables generate varied enemy compositions

world_building_techniques: Environmental exploration of Phlan port city and surrounding corrupted areas
NPC dialogue and parley system where alignment affects interactions
Monster depictions drawn directly from AD&D Monster Manual for lore consistency
Setting placed in Forgotten Realms campaign world
Quest structure: clear out marauding inhabitants, confront evil leader

boss_design.philosophy: Culminating encounter representing the pinnacle of the evil forces threatening Phlan; positioned as narrative endpoint after progressive area clearing
notable_bosses: The powerful leader of the evil forces (final encounter; specific identity not detailed in source)

faction_indicators: Character alignment (good/neutral/evil) affects NPC perception and interaction
Party composition (race/class) may influence faction relationships in Phlan
Settled vs. unsettled portions of Phlan establish power dynamics
danger_signals: Marauding inhabitants occupying areas surrounding Phlan
Escalating enemy encounters as party progresses toward final confrontation
Corrupted dungeon areas requiring systematic clearing
environmental_storytelling: First-person exploration perspective reveals dungeon layout and enemy placement
Visual sprite depictions at short/medium/long range create distance-appropriate threat perception
Top-down isometric combat view reveals tactical positioning of enemies and allies
Port city setting of Phlan establishes geography and trade hub importance
Graveyard and other locations carry thematic significance for exploration
